findings.title: Fail-first provenance remains explicit verification debt for this behavior-defining hardening slice
findings.rationale: The bounded package explicitly states that preserved fail-first/red-run artifacts are not available because normalization happened after hardening was already in flight, and relies on brownfield regression coverage plus corrected-baseline reruns as the substitute. The current assertion map and focused, impacted-auth, and full-suite reruns materially reduce closure risk, but they do not fully eliminate retrofit-risk for a security-sensitive contract change because the package cannot prove the new tests would have failed before the implementation.
findings.suggested_action: Keep this gap recorded as explicit accepted verification debt unless a preserved red-run artifact can be reconstructed; do not reopen the implementation based on this finding alone because the corrected-baseline package provides strong substitute coverage and a green full Laravel CI-equivalent rerun.

overall_assessment: The corrected-baseline package presents strong current-state coverage for RR-AUTH-04 and does not show brittle test-only shortcuts, mock fallbacks, or weak payload assertions in the documented proof surfaces. Test quality is acceptable for rerun closure review, with one material residual concern: the package still lacks preserved fail-first evidence for a behavior-defining security hardening slice.
recommended_path: Accept the corrected-baseline rerun as substantively sufficient on test effectiveness and coverage depth, carry forward the explicit fail-first provenance debt as a recorded residual risk, and continue with the remaining no-context reruns, triple-audit round 02, and final orchestration guards.
